title: Extracting central charge from ground-state overlaps of spatially deformed Hamiltonians
authors: Chen Bai, Xinyu Sun, Liang-Hong Mo, Hong-Hao Tu
abstract: We show that the conformal anomaly of a $(1+1)$-dimensional conformal field theory can be extracted directly from a ground-state wave-function overlap associated with a spatial conformal deformation. Focusing on the $q$-Möbius deformation, we derive an exact overlap formula between the deformed and undeformed ground states, whose exponent depends only on the central charge. Motivated by this result, we construct a lattice estimator based solely on ground-state overlaps and apply it to representative critical quantum chains and the gapless edge modes of a two-dimensional Chern insulator. Numerical results demonstrate that the resulting overlaps provide a simple and robust probe of the central charge in microscopic models. We further demonstrate that the deformed ground states retain universal geometric structures in their entanglement spectra and entanglement entropies. These results provide a simple wave-function-based route to probing conformal data in critical systems and topological edge modes.
summary: This work introduces a novel technique to measure the central charge of a CFT by analyzing the ground-state overlap of a system subjected to a $q$-Möbius spatial deformation. By applying this method to critical 1D chains and 2D topological insulators, the authors show that the overlap provides a simple, robust, and universal probe of fundamental CFT data using only wave-function properties.
main_problem: The central problem is developing a method to extract the central charge ($c$) of a $(1+1)$-dimensional Conformal Field Theory (CFT) using only ground-state overlaps derived from spatially deformed Hamiltonians.
main_result: The ground-state overlap under $q$-Möbius deformations provides a simple and robust, wave-function-based probe for the central charge in critical quantum chains and topological edge modes.
method: The authors derive an exact overlap formula using CFT techniques applied to the $q$-Möbius deformation, and then construct a lattice estimator based on this overlap.
model_or_system: The study applies to gapless 1D quantum systems, including critical chains (Ising, Potts, Heisenberg) and the edge modes of 2D Chern insulators (QWZ model). The Hamiltonian is subjected to a spatially modulated $q$-Möbius deformation.
key_observables: Central charge ($c$), ground-state overlaps ($\langle 0|0_q(	heta) angle$), entanglement spectrum, and entanglement entropy ($S_A$).
important_parameters: Deformation parameters $q$ (modulation wave number) and $	heta$ (deformation strength); system size $L$.
main_assumptions: The method relies on the deformation being generated by a finite conformal transformation to maintain analytical tractability.
figures_summary: Figures demonstrate the finite-size extrapolation of the effective central charge ($c_{	ext{eff}}$) for various critical chains, and show the entanglement spectrum and entropy for deformed Ising and Potts chains, as well as the 2D QWZ model.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from the CFT derivation of the overlap formula, to its lattice realization, and finally applies this estimator to calculate $c$ and analyze entanglement structures for both 1D and 2D topological models.
why_interesting: This provides a powerful, purely wave-function-based diagnostic tool to probe universal CFT properties (like $c$) in complex, interacting quantum many-body systems, which is highly relevant for understanding critical points in condensed matter.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
